stimulus:
While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes:

  • In southern Australia, mallee shrublands have expanded into areas that were once heathlands.
  • Aisha Khan and Liam O'Connor of the University of Adelaide investigated whether mallee expansion is linked to changes in fire regimes.
  • Khan and O'Connor analyzed growth rings from mallee shrubs on a site that was previously heathland and matched the ring data to fire-history records.
  • They found that periods of mallee population growth tended to follow long intervals between major fires.
stem: The student wants to emphasize the aim of the research study. Which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal?

answerOptions.content: Aisha Khan and Liam O'Connor, researchers at the University of Adelaide, wanted to know whether mallee expansion is linked to changes in fire regimes.
Thanks to the work done by Aisha Khan and Liam O'Connor, we now know that long intervals between major fires may have contributed to mallee expansion.
University of Adelaide researchers determined that mallee population growth was associated with longer fire-free intervals.
Aisha Khan and Liam O'Connor analyzed growth rings from mallee shrubs on a site that was previously heathland and matched the ring data to fire-history records.

rationale:
Choice A is the best answer because it states the aim of the study—to investigate whether mallee expansion is linked to fire regimes.

Choices B and C describe findings rather than the aim. Choice D describes methodology, not aim.
